heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 225, which occurred on 28 May 2026 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 18 July 2026 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The ongoing dry stretch, 20 days and counting started on 03 July 2026 and ranks 1st in length since records started in 23 September 2024.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 3 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 27.7°C, observed on 24 April 2026 when the temperature fell to -4.5°C but also managed to reach 23.2°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: Right now, a heatwave is in effect, it started on 2026-07-01 and has continued for 22 days.  There has been 3 heatwaves this year; usually, 1.9 occurs annually.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was 28 June 2026, there have been only 68 tropical nights since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (223°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 128.2 mph from 27 January 2026.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 2.0°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 671 m) and inside humidity (up 6%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 398.5m lower than yesterday, 670.5m lower than the past week, 26.8m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1829m minimum and 6260m maximum, with an average of 3830m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4500m and 3800m.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate mostly high temperatures. Outside; extreme heat is expected, potentially dangerous in the sun. Also, humidity will be quite oppressive and very little to no wind will be present. Chance of light rain, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. Little change in the weather.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
